entry.title: How to Perform Initial Loads from S/4HANA to Advanced Event Mesh with Odata Poll
entry.content:
Since RAP does not have a Initial Load Feature, a simple Cloud Integration Flow is able to fetch the current Data of a System via Odata and pass it to AEM using the AEM Adapter.

Prerequisite is of course that the API is activated on the S/4HANA System.

This is how the Iflow looks like:

 

Since an Init Load only needs to run once, we set the Timer like this:
 

We are calling the Local Integration Flow in a Loop (explained [here](https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-members/sap-cloud-integration-looping-process-call/ba-p/13598555)because it might be possible that there is so much data in the system that one call would return a too large Payload. Using the EDMX File from the SAP Accelerator Hub (https://api.sap.com/api/API_BUSINESS_PARTNER/resource/Business_Partner) the required fields can be selected in a wizard. Additionally we specify how many Records we want:
 

Query Options:

$select=BusinessPartner,BusinessPartnerGrouping,FirstName,LastName,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/AddressID,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/ValidityStartDate,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/ValidityEndDate,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/StreetName,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/CityName,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/HouseNumber,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/PostalCode,to_BusinessPartnerAddress/Country&$expand=to_BusinessPartnerAddress

In the next Step we have a Splitter to create individual Events:

Then we set the Grouping as Property, because we want to set the Topic dynamically

Odata V2 also supports JSON, the Odata V2 Adapter does not. Alternatively you can use the HTTP adapter and build the query yourself. With XML as returned Payload we need a converter:

Last Step: Set up the AEM Adapter. Important here is to use the Grouping Property for the dynamic Topic:

 

Test: For Testing I have added a Consumer Iflow subscribing to Topic sap/businessPartner/> (for all Groupings)

One Example Message:
Headers

Payload

If I change the BP manually, it should appear as well:

Changed the Address
 

--> Result in AEM (This Changed Event is triggered by AIFAEM)  

 

(on the left we see the Payload from the Init Load Iflow, only the order is different, which should be fine. It might be possible that the Odata Pull returns a different Payload than the Event pushed by the ERP. In this case you either need to add a Mapping on Integration Suite or design your Custom Event so it fits to the Odata Payload from the beginning. 

Advantages:

- Clean Approach without any changes in the backend except activation of the Odata API

- Does not matter what Even Enablement is used on S/4HANA. Any Migration between Event Enablement Options does not affect this

Disadvantages:

- Payload has to be aligned with Event when the corresponding Object is changed (if the Init Load goes into the same Topic)

API has to be available, activated and one Iflow for it designed specifically.

entry.title: Re: How to eliminate splits for a particular wage type
entry.content:
Means Lets say Basic is 1000 in the month of April due to split 500 and 500 is happening you want CPF amount on whole 1000 We can pass the amount of basic and other wage types in a variable and do the calculation of CPF for bonus wage types

But we need to look the below

1. What in case if there is CPF is eligible for the employee from non-bonus wage types.

2. If the above scenario exists what happens is we need to cumulate the final amount of CPF from bonus wage types and non-bonus wage types in final RT so in Statutory reports the total of CPF will be (bonus + non bonus wage types)

3. Can you share how the CPF is calculated now for bonus employee Is my understanding is correct

entry.title: Re: Rewards & Recognition: Guidelines formula
entry.content:
Hello @AndresCarter,

Thank you so much for your help. I'm gonna to share all the steps that I perform in order to achieve the request:

1. Make sure that the advanced setting "Can nominators override the recommended award amount?" on Spot Award Program is enable. 

2. Create a rule to make the calculations and link with the MDF Spot Award Object.

NOTE: this rule is linked in Save Rule, but it was calculated 2 times. For that reason, I have added a condition to block the 2nd multiplication.

3. The last step, was to create a new Business Rule to make an interval between 2%-10%.

I hope that this helps you too @MeghaSadhotra. 🙂

Ana Lousada

entry.title: Re: How to eliminate splits for a particular wage type
entry.content:
If a wage type is a CPF wage, it is marked as either

/102-OW for CPF or

/103-AW for CPF in V_512W_D.

And there is no issue in CPF calculations if staff is a Permanent resident or a Citizen for the full payroll month (IT0186 fund type '01') OR full month exempt (for foreigners IT0186 '04')

The issue is only for part month IT186 change. For example, if a foreign staff is converted to permanent resident on 11th of the month, whatever pay he receive for first 10 days is exempt from CPF, and CPF will be calculated for next 20 days. For example, the full month pay of $3,000 is split into two. $1,000 (for 10 days) and $2,000 (for 20 days) accordingly. and $2,000 is subjected to CPF which is deriving correctly as per standard rules. 

The same logic should not be applicable to bonus. For example, the full month bonus of $3,000 is split into two. $1,000 (for 10 days) and $2,000 (for 20 days) accordingly. But all $3,000 should be subjected to CPF, if a foreigner is converted to PR/citizen in IT186 on or before 15th of the month and No CPF if conversion happens after 15th. We need a custom solution for this.

Hope this clarifies. Thanks

entry.title: Re: Customer COE: SAP EarlyWatch Alert transitions from SAP Solution Manager to SAP for Me
entry.content:
Hello Matias,

thank you for your question! Non-VAR customer can send the SAP EarlyWatch Alert raw data directly to SAP from their systems without SAP Solution Manager and without SAP Cloud ALM. The procedure is described in the section "Solution" in SAP Note 207223.

SAP Note 3391480  explains that SAP EarlyWatch Alert is available in SAP for Me and not implemented in SAP Cloud ALM. The only topic which I mentioned in my blog above is, that it was planned to include a set-up procedure in SAP Cloud ALM that semi-automates the steps mentioned in SAP Note 207223. 

Using the SAP EarlyWatch Alert service is always possible, independent from SAP Solution Manager, SAP Focused Run and SAP Cloud ALM.

I hope this answers your question!

Best regards,

Susanne

entry.title: How to perform Initial Loads & Bulk Init Loads in AIFAEM triggered by an ABAP Program
entry.content:
Introduction

In my previous Blogs about AIFAEM I demonstrated how to create Custom AIFAEM Events and trigger them by Creating or Changing a Business Partner in Transaction BP. When an AEM Broker is introduced into a Landscape, Customers might want to send out all existing Objects for an Event to the Broker without having to do Mass Changes to the Object (which would trigger the individual Events created before). Also due to Performance It might be necessary to send out the Events as Bulk Messages. Both approaches are explained in this Blog

 

Trigger Program

For triggering the Events there is no Out-of-the-Box Feature in AIFAEM, so we will need a Custom Program which can be triggered by a User.

This is how it could be done:

 

*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*& Report ZAIF_BUPA_INIT_LOAD
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
*&
*&---------------------------------------------------------------------*
REPORT zaif_bupa_init_load.

DATA: gt_swcont          TYPE TABLE OF swcont,
      gv_businesspartner TYPE bu_partner,
      gt_events          TYPE TABLE OF /aif/event_raw,
      gt_bulk            TYPE TABLE OF zaif_event_raw_table_structure,
      gv_counter         TYPE i,
      gv_objtype         TYPE swo_objtyp VALUE 'BUS1006'.

SELECT-OPTIONS s_bp FOR gv_businesspartner.

PARAMETERS p_blk AS CHECKBOX TYPE abap_boolean.
PARAMETERS p_pksz TYPE i DEFAULT '1000'.

START-OF-SELECTION.

  "Get IDs
  SELECT businesspartner
      FROM a_businesspartner
      INTO TABLE (gt_businesspartners)
      WHERE businesspartner IN @s_bp.

  LOOP AT gt_businesspartners ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<gs_businesspartner>).
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO gt_events ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<gs_event>).
    <gs_event>-event = 'INIT'.
    <gs_event>-objkey = CONV swo_typeid( <gs_businesspartner> ).
    <gs_event>-objtype = gv_objtype.
    <gs_event>-rectype = 'ZAIF_EVENT'.
  ENDLOOP.

  "Send to AIF
  IF p_blk IS INITIAL. "Single Events
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD /aif/cl_enabler_xml=>transfer_to_aif_mult
          EXPORTING
            it_any_structure = gt_events
            iv_queue_ns      = 'AEM'
            iv_queue_name    = '001'.

      CATCH cx_root INTO DATA(lx_root).
        MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
    ENDTRY.
  ELSE. "Bulk
    "Build Packages
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO gt_bulk ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_bulk>).
    <ls_bulk>-objtype = gv_objtype.
    LOOP AT gt_events ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_event>).
      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_bulk>-events ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_bulk_event>).
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING <ls_event> TO <ls_bulk_event>.
      gv_counter = gv_counter + 1.
      IF gv_counter = p_pksz AND sy-tabix < lines( gt_events ).
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO gt_bulk ASSIGNING <ls_bulk>.
        <ls_bulk>-objtype = gv_objtype.
        CLEAR gv_counter.
      ENDIF.
    ENDLOOP.

    "Send Bulk to AIF
    TRY.
        CALL METHOD /aif/cl_enabler_xml=>transfer_to_aif_mult
          EXPORTING
            it_any_structure = gt_bulk
            iv_queue_ns      = 'AEM'
            iv_queue_name    = '001'.

      CATCH cx_root INTO lx_root.
        MESSAGE lx_root->get_text( ) TYPE 'E'.
    ENDTRY.
  ENDIF.

 

This Program already is able to do both Individual Events and Bulk.

We have 1 Select Option:

Business Partner: Selection of Business Partners which need to be triggered. If all BPs need to be triggered, just leave it empty.

Additionally there are two Parameters:

Send as Bulk: Activate if you want to send out multiple BPs in a Bulk Message

Objects per Bulk Message: Specify how many BPs should be in one Bulk Message

Why are we not using the FM /AIF/TRANSFER_TO_AIF_RECEIVER?

We are passing the Messages to AIF via Class Method /aif/cl_enabler_xml=>transfer_to_aif_mult , because this Method enables us to pass multiple messages to AIF at once. This way we can use the Parallelization, where AIF schedules the Messages in Jobs. In /N/AIF/PERS_CGR we are able to specify the Number of Messages per Job for our Config Group AEM / 001

If we, for instance, trigger 10.000 individual Event Messages to AIF, AIF will plan 10 Jobs with 1.000 AIF Messages each. They will be processed in Jobs in the Background parallely. 

 

AIF Setup

Individual Events

For individual Init Load Events we use the existing AIF Interface set up in this Blog.

Bulk Events

Since the Source Structure /AIF/EVENT_RAW of BOR Events can only hold one Identifier for an Event, we will need another Event which can hold a List of Identifiers (in this case Business Partner Numbers). Therefore I created a new Source Structure 

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Structure with Array of AIF Events'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
define structure zaif_event_raw_table_structure {

  events  : zaif_event_raw_t;
  objtype : swo_objtyp;

}

 

zaif_event_raw_t is a Table Type for /AIF/EVENT_RAW

The Target Structure of course also needs to be an Array of Business Partners instead of one Business Partner:

 

@EndUserText.label : 'Event Message'
@AbapCatalog.enhancement.category : #NOT_EXTENSIBLE
define structure zaif_aem_bupa_event_message {

  controller   : /aif/aem_controller;
  data         : zaif_aem_bupa;
  ignore       : abap_boolean;
}

 

zaif_aem_bupa_tt = Table Type for zaif_aem_bupa (Data Component of Event in this Blog)

Now I can define my Interface:

Do not forget to set up the Interface Engines to XML and maintain the Interface Determination like in AEM / BUPA in my previous Blog.

The Structure Mapping is similar to AEM / BUPA, except that the Topic is different

Also the Function Module to fetch the Data for the BPs is placed in the CONTROLLER-Structure Mapping (then I only need one Structure Mapping) and changed due to different Source and Target Structure + I select the Data for all BPs with one Select Statement per CDS View.

 

FUNCTION zaif_aem_bupa_get_data_bulk .
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------
*"*"Local Interface:
*"  IMPORTING
*"     REFERENCE(RAW_STRUCT) TYPE  ZAIF_EVENT_RAW_TABLE_STRUCTURE
*"     REFERENCE(RAW_LINE)
*"     REFERENCE(SMAP) TYPE  /AIF/T_SMAP
*"     REFERENCE(INTREC) TYPE  /AIF/T_INTREC
*"     REFERENCE(SENDING_SYSTEM) TYPE  /AIF/AIF_BUSINESS_SYSTEM_KEY
*"       OPTIONAL
*"  TABLES
*"      RETURN_TAB STRUCTURE  BAPIRET2 OPTIONAL
*"  CHANGING
*"     REFERENCE(OUT_STRUCT) TYPE  ZAIF_AEM_BUPA_EVENT_MSG_BULK
*"     REFERENCE(DEST_LINE)
*"     REFERENCE(DEST_TABLE)
*"     REFERENCE(APPEND_FLAG) TYPE  C
*"----------------------------------------------------------------------

  DATA lt_bupas TYPE TABLE OF but0id.

  LOOP AT out_struct-data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_out_data>).
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO lt_bupas ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_bupa>).
    <ls_bupa>-partner = <ls_out_data>-businesspartnertype-businesspartner.
  ENDLOOP.

  IF lt_bupas IS NOT INITIAL.
    SELECT
      FROM a_businesspartner
      FIELDS
        businesspartner,
        businesspartnergrouping,
        firstname,
        lastname,
        businesspartnercategory
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN _bupas
     WHERE businesspartner = _bupas-partner
      INTO TABLE (lt_bp_data).

    SELECT
      FROM a_businesspartneraddress
      FIELDS
      businesspartner,
      addressid,
      validitystartdate,
      validityenddate,
      streetname,
      cityname,
      housenumber,
      postalcode,
      country
      FOR ALL ENTRIES IN _bupas
      WHERE businesspartner = _bupas-partner
      INTO TABLE (lt_bp_address_data).
  ENDIF.

  IF lt_bp_data IS NOT INITIAL.
    "First clean up Target Structure
    CLEAR out_struct-data.
    "Fill with Data from CDS Views
    LOOP AT lt_bp_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_bp_data>).
      APPEND INITIAL LINE TO out_struct-data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_data>).
      MOVE-CORRESPONDING <ls_bp_data> TO <ls_data>-businesspartnertype.
      LOOP AT lt_bp_address_data ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_bp_address_data>) WHERE businesspartner = <ls_bp_data>-businesspartner.
        APPEND INITIAL LINE TO <ls_data>-businesspartnertype-to_bpaddress-bpaddress ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_address>).
        MOVE-CORRESPONDING <ls_bp_address_data> TO <ls_address>.
      ENDLOOP.
    ENDLOOP.
  ELSE.
    CALL FUNCTION '/AIF/UTIL_ADD_MSG'
      EXPORTING
        msgty      = 'E'
        msgid      = '/AIF/MES'
        msgno      = '000'
        msgv1      = 'No Business partner found'
      TABLES
        return_tab = return_tab.

  ENDIF.

ENDFUNCTION.

The assigned Action of course also is the same

 

Test

Individual Events

Result in /N/AIF/ERR

Bulk Events

Result in AIF:

What if I build Packages with up to 5 Events per Package?

 

Result in AIF:

 

Results in BTP Integration Suite:

First Message has 5 Business Partners:

Second Message 2:

Filtering

In one of my previous Blogs I have introduced you to Filtering in AIFAEM. In a Bulk interface the approach must be different, because we cannot decide to send or not to send a Message based on the BP Group, because there are multiple Groups inside of the message. So we need to filter out the BPs inside of AIF before sending the Events out to AEM. Still we can use the same Value Mapping. 

For this I create a Field Mapping with Sub-Table (indirect Mapping) 

This means that for every EVENTS-Record in the Source Side a DATA-Record is created on the Target Side, when I create a Structure Mapping for them. This is what i did:

For the Filtering I will need 2 fields:

1) Filter Criteria: Business Partner Grouping. To be able to filter on it, I´ll need to fetch it first. That is why i created a Field Mapping for it:


 Updating Media

With the Value Mapping BUPA_GET_GROUP I can fetch the Data from the Database:

2) Filter Flag: Since we are working inside of the DATA-Object, I don´t want to add a "IGNORE"-Field. Instead I will use the Field FIRSTNAME. The actual Value from the BP will overwrite the IGNORE-Information later in the Funtion Module, which select the actual Data, anyway.

After clicking the F4-Help for Field Name 1 we can switch to the Target Structure to use the Target Field BUSINESSPARTNERGROUPING (Button "Switch Structure")

This means, that if the Grouping is not maintained in the Value Mapping, it will write "X" into FIRSTNAME.

Then in the same Structure Mapping I add a Check that ensures that the Row is only mapped if FIRSTNAME is empty

The actual Value for FIRSTNAME is mapped in the Function Module here

 

The EVENTS->DATA Structure Mapping is an indirect mapping. AIF processes mappings recursively, so the lower levels before the higher levels. This means that the actual values of FIRSTNAME are mapped at the end. Here one example:

Lets assume we have these 5 Business partners selected by the Program. Without the Filter the Mapping Result is like this:

In my value mapping i only allow 0001:

I transform the Data and see this as result: Only the 0001 Group is mapped.

That´s it! Happy Eventing 😉

entry.title: Bank Opening Date Matters? [Incorrect bank opening date in Bank master affects anything?]
entry.content:
Hi,

In SAP S/4 HANA private cloud edition, I registered  Bank account and account ID in NWBC, but registered a wrong bank opening date (Wrong: 01.04.2025, Correct: 01.08.2017). But IBAN valid from is correct (01.08.2017). This is during the process of configuration for F110 (Automatic payment) on a test environment for the purpose of users' UAT, not production environment.

After completing the above Bank account registration, I noticed the wrong opening date, and tried to edit the opening date but the field is not editable, which is controlled by HQ IT.

My question is as following.

1. Is there any possible negative effect/possible by leaving the opening date (01.04.2025) rather than changing it to the correct date? *IBAN Valid from is correct.

2. If yes, what effect or issue would be expected?

Your kind and prompt support/comment would be very much appreciated.

Thank you.

SAP S/4HANA SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

entry.title: the Datetime/DatetimeOffset format for filtering data when executing the API call
entry.content:
Dear Community,

I am currently experiencing issues with filtering a date-type entity when executing the API call. 

I tried these formats on the API Purchase Order(ODataV4) to filter data using the Try Out feature on Business Accelerator Hub.

$filter=LastChangeDateTime gt '2020-05-20T00:00:00Z'

$filter=LastChangeDateTime gt datetimeoffset'2020-05-20T00:00:00Z'

$filter=LastChangeDateTime gt datetimeoffset'2020-05-20T00:00:00.000Z'

However, none of them worked, and I received this error message.

{
  "error": {
    "code": "005056A509B11EE1BB8AF4A65EC3CA20",
    "message": {
      "lang": "en",
      "value": "Invalid parametertype used at function 'gt' (Position: 14)"
    },
    "innererror": {
      "transactionid": "600414ADECD10160E0067EE577579611",
      "timestamp": "20250404072147.4033660",
      "Error_Resolution": {
        "SAP_Transaction": "For backend administrators: use ADT feed reader \"SAP Gateway Error Log\" or run transaction /IWFND/ERROR_LOG on SAP Gateway hub system and search for entries with the timestamp above for more details",
        "SAP_Note": "See SAP Note 1797736 for error analysis (https://service.sap.com/sap/support/notes/1797736)"
      }
    }
  }
}

Please can someone tell me how I can filter data-type entities? What format is correct?

I'm not sure but I heard Datetime is not available for OData4, so if there is any way to filter data-type entities, I would appreceate it if you gave me some advice!!

 

Thanks.

Regards,

entry.title: Converting Excel File to XML format in SAP CPI using JAR files and groovy script
entry.content:
Hello Sapians,

Sometimes we encountered an issue where the third party is sending the file in Excel format and we have to read that file, convert to XML and process in SAP CPI. As we are familiar we don't have any direct function available as of now to convert excel file to XML. To overcome this issue, below are the step by step process to implement in CPI to read and convert Excel file.

This converter is based on Apache POI 3.17 library. In order for conversion the following JAR files need to be referenced/imported into the project.

 

  • commons-collections4-4.1.jar  (https://jar-download.com/artifacts/org.apache.commons/commons-collections4/4.1/source-code)
  • converter-core-2.3.0.jar (https://jar-download.com/artifact-search/converter)
  • poi-3.17.jar (https://jar-download.com/artifacts/org.apache.poi/poi/3.17/source-code)
  • poi-ooxml-3.17.jar (https://jar-download.com/artifacts/org.apache.poi/poi-ooxml/3.17/source-code)
  • poi-ooxml-schemas-3.17.jar (https://jar-download.com/?search_box=poi-ooxml-schemas-3.17)
  • xmlbeans-2.6.0.jar (https://jar-download.com/?search_box=xmlbeans-2.6.0&p=2)

The library files can be downloaded from above mentioned links or Apache website.

Sample Excel file in .xsls format.

To import the JAR files, Go to 

<<IFlow>> --> Click on blank panel (you will get Integration Flow) --> References --> Add --> Archive

Select the required Jar files from your system and click on Add.

Import all the above mentioned JAR files to your Iflow.

Sender Adapter is configured with HTTPS adapter.

Add the content modifier, as we have to configure some parameters in exchange properties.

 

 

Content Modifier properties :

1. converterClass : com.equalize.converter.core.Excel2XMLConverter 

2. sheetName : << Specify the sheet Name that need to be process from your Excel File>>

3. processFieldNames : <<The header fields  will be processed from the file>>

4. documentName : << Specify the name in which the XML header tag to be generated>>

5. documentNamespace : << Specify the name in which the XML namespace to be generated>>

 

To understand more about parameters that can be added, you can check below parameter reference.

Below is a list of the parameters for configuration Excel to XML conversion. Certain parameters will automatically inherit the default values if it is not configured.

Parameter Name Allowed values Default value Remarks

converterClassPI - com.equalize.xpi.af.modules.excel.Excel2XMLTransformer
CPI - com.equalize.converter.core.Excel2XMLConverter		 Required field. Determines conversion class.
sheetName  The name of the active Excel sheet to extract. Either sheetName or sheetIndex must be populated.
sheetIndexInteger values beginning from 0 The index of the active Excel sheet to extract (starts from 0). Either sheetName or sheetIndex must be populated.
skipEmptyRowsY, NYEmpty rows to be skipped or not
rowOffsetInteger values beginning from 10Starting row to begin extracting content from (i.e. 0 = start from first row, 1 = start from second row). If processFieldNames = 'fromFile' and rowOffset = 0, first line will be automatically be headerRow+1
headerRowInteger values beginning from 10Available only when processFieldNames = 'fromFile'. Determines which row to retrieve header column names from (0-based so 0 is first row, 1 is second row)
columnOffsetInteger values beginning from 10Starting column to begin extracting content from (i.e. 0 = start from first column, 1 = start from second column)
processFieldNamesfromFile, fromConfiguration, notAvailable Required field. Determines the naming of each column of the rows, and the number of columns to extract:

    • fromFile = Column names and number of columns are determined from header line of the sheet

    • fromConfiguration = Column names and number of columns are determined from parameter fieldNames

    • notAvailable = Column names will be set as ColumnX, where X = 1,2,3,4. Number of columns will be determined from parameter columnCount


fieldNames  Name of columns. Required field when processFieldNames = 'fromConfiguration'
onlyValidCharsInXMLNameY, NNAvailable only when processFieldNames = 'fromFile'. Removes invalid XML characters from column names retrieved from header row of sheet
columnCountInteger values beginning from 1 Number of columns for extraction. Required field when processFieldNames = 'notAvailable'
recordName RecordXML element name for row of record in output
documentName  Required field. Document name of root element of XML output
documentNamespace  Required field. Namespace of root element of XML output
formattingexcel, rawexcelControls how the cell contents are formatted in XML output

    • excel = Cells are displayed the same way as Excel formatting of corresponding cell

    • raw = Raw value of cells are displayed


evaluateFormulasY, NYControls how cell contents with formulas are displayed in XML output

    • YES = Cells are displayed with result of formula evalution

    • NO = Cells are displayed with actual formula


emptyCellOutputsuppress, defaultValuesuppressControls how empty cells are displayed in XML output

    • suppress = Empty cells are not displayed (no corresponding XML tags for empty cells)

    • defaultValue = Empty cells will be displayed with default value


emptyCellDefaultValue <blank>If emptyCellOutput = 'defaultValue', all empty cells will be populated with value in this parameter
indentFactorInteger values beginning from 10Determines the number of indentation spaces for each level in the XML output
debugY, NNDisplays contents in Audit Log of each cell extracted. WARNING: Use this only for debugging in non-productive systems

 

After that add the groovy script to convert the file to XML.

Groovy Script : 

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

import com.sap.gateway.ip.core.customdev.util.Message
import com.equalize.cpi.converter.FormatConversionBean
 
def Message processData(Message message) {
 
def fcb = new FormatConversionBean(message.exchange, message.getProperties())
def output = fcb.convert()
message.setBody(output)
return message
}
 
-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Add content modifier to read the XML generated.

 

To test the scenario, we are testing through postman.

Add you file in binary format.

This conclude the blog, the conversion of Excel to XML file😊.

In case of any queries, you can comment in the blog. Will try to resolve your concerns.

entry.title: Re: How to set default Tax Codes for Customer Invoice, Cr...
entry.content:
Hello,

 

You can configure external tax events/tax code determination with the fine tuning activity External Tax Determination for Goods and Services in you Business Configuration:

The External Tax Determination for Goods and Services business option allows you to create and edit parameters for the external determination of incoming and outgoing tax events.

The External Tax Determination for Goods and Services business option is relevant if you wish to be able to overwrite the tax events determined by the system using the standard tax decision tree, for example, for certain scenarios where you may want to use local tax codes.

 

  1. Go to the Business Configuration Work Center.
  2. Go to the Implementation Projects View.
  3. Select your Implementation Project and click the Open Activity List button.
  4. Go to the Fine-Tune Tab.
  5. Search and open the External Tax Determination for Goods and Services activity.
  6. Create a configuration entry including if required and the Tax Code Details accordingly.

 

 

See also the following for more information:

KBA 2332151 - Configure Tax Event / Tax Code Determination for Special Use Cases

Blog: https://community.sap.com/t5/enterprise-resource-planning-blogs-by-sap/external-tax-determination-for-goods-and-services-in-sap-business-bydesign/ba-p/13389125

 

Best regards,

Kathrin

entry.title: Re: Order is blocked by GTS, Invoice possible
entry.content:
Hello,

When a sales order is blocked by SAP Global Trade Services (GTS), it typically affects the creation of outbound deliveries. In the scenario described, the invoice for service items can still be created even if the sales order is blocked by GTS.

Generally, GTS checks compliance on sales orders and blocks delivery if there’s a compliance issue. Sales in SAP S/4HANA Cloud 2111 The creation of invoices for service items doesn’t necessarily depend on delivery blocks, especially if they are processed differently, such as order-related billing without requiring an outbound delivery.

To specifically prevent invoicing for service items when a sales order is blocked by GTS, you would need to review invoice creation routines and ensure that they are aligned with GTS checks. This might involve setting up specific business logic or checks within your SAP configuration.

Usually, you can configure blocking reasons for various processes within SAP, but the direct prevention of invoice creation due to a compliance block is not standard behavior for service items. You might need to develop a custom solution or implement a manual check to ensure service items are not invoiced under blocked conditions unless specifically configured otherwise.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Billing document -PDF Extract
entry.content:
Hello,

To mass download invoices in PDF format using SAP S/4HANA Cloud, you can follow a process that utilizes SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation (RPA) for automating invoice downloading tasks. Here are some steps you can consider:

Approach using SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation SAP Intelligent Robotic Process Automation Content (RPA):

  1. Utilize SAP Intelligent RPA Bots: Leverage SAP Intelligent RPA bots, which can automate the process of downloading multiple invoices in PDF format. These bots can be configured to interact with the SAP system and manage batch requests to download PDFs of billing documents.

  2. Use the ‘Mass Download of Billing Documents Output for Commercial Projects’ 3208892 - How to mass download PDF invoices - S/4HANA Cloud Feature: You can use the SAP functionality designed for mass downloading invoices as part of commercial projects using SAP Intelligent RPA. This method involves configuring bots to perform bulk actions, thereby saving time and improving efficiency.

  3. Integration with Cloud Studio and Automation: Set up automation processes using SAP Cloud Studio to initiate and manage batch requests for multiple invoices. This allows you to process numerous documents seamlessly.

Best regards,

Dequan Xu

entry.title: Re: Service broker error: Service broker destination-serv...
entry.content:
Hello All,

Here is a clarification of the root cause which i have received from SAP:


In order to optimize the runtime performance of HTML5 apps looking up destination in HTML5 enabled instances of the Destination service, some changes in the internal structure of the data were introduced. These changes initially were released as a mixture of both the old and new structure. In order to migrate fully away from the old structure (and thus applying the optimization for everyone), a one-time migration of anything left on the old structure was performed. However, this migration had a flaw which removed some of the technical permissions of the migrated objects, making it forbidden for the Destination service broker component to modify them.
Thus, once an update was made which would prompt the broker component to interact with these objects, the observed error occurred.

Hence this was the issue from SAP itself.

Solution:

In order to fix this, SAP applied a one-time operation to restore the technical permission to the migrated objects.

(Reaching to SAP solved the issue.)

Regards,

Mahendra Saini

entry.title: A letter to Otto – PM of the EarlyWatch Alert. I love you Otto
entry.content:
Dear Otto,

I'm writing this letter, although I suspect you might never read it, you might be gratefully retired in Mallorca or Turks and Caicos, with my heartfelt thank you.

We never met, but I've been working with SAP systems for many years, and let me tell you something, everything lives and dies really fast in SAP, everything except the EWA. We must recognize it, your product is incredible, lasting impact of the meteorite that killed the dinosaurs and created SAP, and the world was created.

I still remember the first time I saw your report, I am not that old so it was mid 2000s, but your EWA report felt revolutionary. I was looking for your instructions, and they were given in a SIM file.

You remember SIM? it was a kind of a video, SIMulator, that SAP gave as instructions, because Youtube didn't exist. Otto, given the complexities, you communicated with us, your messages were heard. No interactions, but the community existed.

Otto's Business Plan, I didnt need more

 

Otto, enough said, we were in the business with you!

Wait a minute

I will do what I can, give me a few years and I catch up

Your report was optimized for Word Perfect 6.0 but... Perfect

I gotta say, all in blue, black and white seemed in line to the user experience of mySAP.com, it was enough for us, because the data was 101% accurate, not a comma could be taken out.

All this has evolved, See how it looks today

130 pages of color graphics! pure gold, factual facts. Germany engineering at its best, hard to find these days.

Yes Otto, All changed in my SAP life... except your weekly emails. SAP asks me to log in a portal called SAP for Me (yes, there are still "Portals"), but I still receive your emails in my inbox, for those that still have inbox.

My ask to you - Help the PM of "Readiness Check"

Otto, we need your help, if you were Bruce Wayne, I would turn my Batman Sign high enough to catch your attention.

The world needs your help with the "SAP Readiness Check" for S/4HANA.

In 2025, 20+ years after you created that incredible thing, we are using another report from SAP for assessing the readiness of a system for a major migration.

I have to be honest, Otto, sometimes interacting with the Readiness Check feels... well, let's just say it doesn't quite have the elegance and clarity that EWA achieved. Looks like this.

Welcome to 2025

 

Otto, your UX intuition is needed. This makes me wonder – wouldn't it be fantastic if you, or at least the principles you embedded into EWA, could somehow connect you with the current SAP Product Manager overseeing the Readiness Check? Perhaps some of that EWA magic – that focus on clarity, actionable insights, and user-friendly presentation of complex data – could help refine Readiness Check into the indispensable tool we need to bring our customers back to the future?

Maybe you were ahead of your time, but You, Otto, were a pioneer of automated, data-driven, factual reporting long before "AI" became the dominant buzzword.

We talk so much today about Artificial Intelligence generating reports and insights, but you built a system that did exactly that, reliably and effectively, using smart logic, deep system knowledge, and deterministic analysis nearly 25 years ago.

So, Otto, wherever you are, thank you. Thank you for your vision, for your contribution to the SAP ecosystem, and for creating a product that has stood the test of time and helped countless professionals keep their SAP systems healthy. SAP EarlyWatch Alert is more than just a report; it's a legacy of pragmatic innovation.

With deepest respect and gratitude,

Mario

entry.title: Re: Step-by-Step Instructions for Effortless Electronic Documents Implementation with TCI
entry.content:
Hello @mate_csak , 

Thanks you for this very useful explanation ! 

We are currently installing the TCI:  3545430 - Transport-Based Correction Instructions (TCI) for the Electronic Document Processing Framework 
Simplifying SAP Note Implementation with TCI for E... - SAP Community

At step 4 we have the dialog indicating whether rollback is possible or not. Unfortunately, for the moment, this is not the case on our side. We have verified that all the prerequisites indicated in the pdf of the note 2187425 - Information about SAP Note Transport based Correction Instructions (TCI) - SAP for Me

The PDF states: If you update the system or if you import a transport request where already the TCI is implemented, you cannot roll back the TCI. (page 19)

We currently have Framework 36 manually installed, as well as the Peppol solution (core,germany peppol and email, belgium). Unfortunately, Framework is not properly installed and old notes are not implemented (following the first installation of the framework in 2020) that's why we want to install the TCI to be on a clean base. This TCI was never installed before. 

Does the fact of already having the Framework and Peppol in the system mean that the TCI considers this to be an “update” and therefore rollback is not possible?

We prefer to secure the installation of this TCI since it will overwrite everything that has been implemented manually. We'll also make a backup of the dev system, but if rollback can be active, that would be better.

If you have any other advice for this situation, we'd certainly appreciate it.

Thanks for your help.

Best regards

Guillaume.

entry.title: Alumni Experience- 1H2025 Release
entry.content:
Alumni Experience Blog has been created together with @niharika and @anantapalli 

The Alumni Experience gives former employees access to their personal and payroll related information after their employment ends. 

In this Blog, we are going to cover on below to have smooth Alumni Experience:

A) Pre-Requisite.

B) Configurations Enablement. 

C)Demo + IAS Sync.

D)Alumni Offboarding Purge.

E) FAQ.

 

A)Pre-Requisite:

1.Enable Offboarding and ensure that the Employee Data Review Step is configured in your Offboarding process variant.

2.Identity Authentication Services (IAS)/IPS should be enabled.

3.Company logo and setting - Enable latest people profile.

4.Company logo and setting- Enable Show inactive user in people profile search.

5.ECP (Employee Central Payroll) should be enabled.

6.Personal Email Should be maintained in Employee profile.

B)Configurations Enablement:

Step1: Feature Enablement

Enable alumni switch from Admin Centre-Onboarding General setting page (it will appear only if offboarding switch is enabled)

Step 2: RBP Enablement 

Once feature is enabled from onboarding general setting page, "Alumni External User" role will be auto-created with user type as "Alumni"

List of Permissions available for RBP:

Assignment: Grant themselves so that alumni can access only their profile.

 

Step 3: Business Rules Enablement

Business Rule Scenarios (2 scenarios) will be auto created once alumni feature enabled:

A)Initiate Alumni Process -It will determine whether terminated employee (offboarded) will be Eligible as Alumni.

B)Set End Date for Alumni User Access: It will set an expiration date to end user's access to alumni experience.

Step 4: Provisioning Jobs Enablement

2 Jobs Need to be enabled :

A) Job Type-ONB2ConvertTerminatedEmployeeToAlumniJob : It will convert Terminate employee to Alumni Status.

Former employees are converted to alumni users on the day after their employment ends.

B)Job Type-ONB2AlumniProcessCloseJob- It will convert back user status from alumni - Terminated status.

When the defined Alumni Access time period is over and once the conversion job runs successfully, alumni user access to the alumni portal is terminated.

Step 5: Job Scheduler Enablement

Job Type-Sync Alumni Data -To ensure that changes to actions or business rules are incorporated into the existing Alumni process, configure the Alumni Maintenance Job.

You've scheduled a one-time maintenance job in the Schedule Job Manager. When triggered, the job updates the alumni process with any changes to actions or business rules. Once configured, this job can be manually triggered as- needed from the Job Scheduler page.

Step 6: Configure Alumni Activation Email Template in IAS

Go to SAP Cloud Identity Services-Identity Provisioning Target Systems (your Identity Authentication target system Transformations)-Switch to JSON Editor-Edit and add below piece of code to transformation xml:

Create a new property called ias.alumni.email.template.id.attribute. Go to SAP Cloud Identity Services Applications & Resources Email Template Sets (select the Email Template Set) On-Behalf Registration (tab) and copy the URL value in your browser right after Template Sets.

After the On-Behalf Registration email template set and new property value have been configured, specify that this email template set should be used for Alumni.
Go to SAP Cloud Identity Services Identity Provisioning Target Systems (your Identity Authentication target system)-Transformations-Switch to JSON Editor-Edit and add below piece of code to transformation xml:

 

C)DEMO+IAS Sync:

Step1: Terminating employee Suzhal M Shocks with end date as 24-feb-25.

Step 2: Employee Data Review Step should be completed and Alumni process will be triggered . 

In order to verify it : Please navigate to Admin centre-manage data - You will observe new object "AlumniProcess" will be created with process status as "Not Started".

Step 3: Start date of Alumni is 25-feb (which is post termination date ) .so if job now runs in provisioning it will convert terminated employee to Alumni status (ONB2ConvertTerminatedEmployeeToAlumni) .

ONB2ConvertTerminatedEmployeeToAlumni Job concept : It will pickup all the users whose process status is "Not started " for alumni process and startDate<=particular date where job ran.

It will update status from Not started to Enabled as shown in below screenshot :

Step4 : Alumni User Status is Active Now in SuccessFactors:

 

Step 5: IAS Sync for Suzhal M Shocks

User Staus is currently inactive in IAS side:

Real Time Sync will convert user status from Employee to Alumni with Delta Sync.

User Login Name will be converted to personal Email Address + User Type updated as Alumni from Employee + Status changed from Inactive to Active.

 

Alumni will receive an automated email to personal email address to login:

Alumni Login from Activation Email :

Initially Alumni Need to accept DPCS Statement

Once login ,please click on employee profile

Alumni can edit personal information:

 

Alumni can update payment information:

D)Alumni Offboarding Purge :

The Alumni Process couples with the Offboarding process, so the Offboarding purge needs to be configured to align with the expiration of alumni access.

The alumni process is a child process of the Offboarding process, however the alumni process can remain active after the Offboarding process is completed.

If the Offboarding purge is triggered while the alumni access is active, the Offboarding purge is skipped for the active alumni users. When enabling Alumni Access, you must update your Offboarding purge configuration, so it aligns with the Alumni Access period.

E)FAQ:

1.If Alumni update payment method and what if work flow configured?.
Answer: Yes workflow will be triggered to user to approve it.

2.Is offboardee and alumni user status are same ?
Answer: Alumni status will get changed only after termination date .

3.Alumni can access outstanding offboarding tasks (ex: KT Tasks)?
Answer: Based on permission provided for alumni role.It is upto alumni user would like to perform or not those outstanding tasks.

4.what is status type for alumni ?
Answer: Alumni shares the external user flag 'e'

5.how alumni will login after termination?
Answer: Personal Email address in mandate to be maintained in employee profile before termination date.

6.Where Alumni Email Templated will be configured?
Answer : It will be configured in IAS side . Kindly refer guide for more details in scetion :Configuring the Alumni Activation Email Template in Identity Authentication.

7.if we convert type to alumni via job(convert term employee to alumni) ,whether rehire will be triggered? because status should be f to trigger rehire.
Answer: Rehire is handled for Alumni.

8.Is it necessary for the alumni process creation job to be scheduled after the hris sync job? Also does this job take care of timezone of the user?
Answer: As for the process closure, Alumni process in itself is independent with very less coupling to offboarding. Having it separate and creating this job is one time activity for the customer.

9.Whether Alumni Access can be revoked by Manager from Dashboard?
Answer:Yes ,it can be revoked by manager from offboarding dashboard. Please find below screenshot.

10.How Alumni Access Date is set in Offboarding Dashboard?
Answer:It will be set based on rule :Set End Date for Alumni User Access

 

Alumni Experience for rehires (Rehire with Old Employment and Rehire with New Employment) to be continued in separate Blog .

Happy Learnings 🙂

Thanks,
Gopinath Murugaiyan
IT Technology Senior Consultant.
Workforce Solutions | Corporate Functions & Analytics.
SAP Labs India Private Limited | Whitefield | Bangalore- 560066, India.

entry.title: Water balance and usage report - Test Guide
entry.content:
Story flow Summary

Prerequisites

This section summarizes all the prerequisites for conducting the test in terms of systems, users, master data, organizational data, other test data, and business conditions. Before you begin the testing procedures, make sure that you complete the steps in the referenced document.

 

System Access

 

System

Details

System

  1. Accessible via SAP Fiori launchpad. Your system administrator provides you with the URL to access the various apps assigned to your role.

 

Required Fiori Apps

Roles

Create business roles using the following SAP business role templates and assign them to your test users. 

Alternatively, if available, you can use the following spaces delivered by SAP. You create a space with pages containing predefined essential apps and assign it to the business role. You then assign this business role to your users. 

For more information, refer to How to Create a Business Role from a Template in the product assistance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud.

 

Overview Table

This guide consists of several steps, which are provided in the table below.

 

Test Procedures

This section describes test procedures for each process step in this scope item.

Environmental Technician

Data Collection:

  1. Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as an Environmental Technician role

  2. Choose My Data Collection App.

  3. You can get an overview of all data collections assigned to you.

  4. Data Collection by using Manual Entry

    - Select a data collection from the list to enter the data.

  5. Data Collection by importing a file - 

    • You can import environmental Data into multiple data collection definitions using a predefined template file.

    • Choose the Import Data option, and a window will pop up to import the file.

    • Click on Download Template File for import option, A Standard template (.XLSL) file will download to your Desktop. 

     

    • Fill in the Data in the template as per the instructions in the file.

    • Save the template.

    • Click on Import Data, choose the Browse option, select the File name from your local Desktop, and import it.

    • If the imported data is correct, an imported message will appear on the screen.


    • If the imported data is incorrect, the system will throw errors. Attached some errors for reference (Ex-Date format is not valid).

     


    • Note: The Standard Data Import template contains two sheets (ENV_AMOUNT_MDEF and Table Field Descriptions). The user should enter the data only in the ENV_AMOUNT_MDEF sheet. If the user tries to fill in the data in the Table Field Descriptions sheet and import it into the system, the system will throw the error "File does not contain any records for import."

  6. Data Collection by using Manual Entry: 

    • Choose Edit and enter data values and a comment for pending records.
    • Choose Save. Your entries are saved in the system.

    Note: Repeat the above step to complete all the data collections.

The following are Data collections used in the Water Balance & Usage model

  • Purchased Water (m3)
  • Well Water (m3)
  • Rainfall(mm)
  • Wastewater after treatment to outside facility (m3)
  • Wastewater after treatment to surface water (m3)
  • Stormwater Runoff (m3)
  • Evaporation (m3)
  • Water in-Product (m3)
  • Water to Irrigation-Ground (m3)

 

 

Environmental Manager

Monitor Data

Procedure:

  1. Log on to the SAP Fiori launchpad as an Environmental Manager role.

  2. Choose Monitor Data App. By using Monitor Data App, you can access environmental data records for a specific data collection, calculation, aggregation, or sampling activity.

  3. Once the Monitor Data - Environment Management app opens, the system displays the recently opened monitor data.        

  4. Choose Display by Location and select the Location from Location Structure. Location-related data will be displayed on the screen.

     

    Click on the title to which you want to see the graphic representation of Collected data, Calculated data, or aggregated data.

  5. Once you click on the Title option, it will navigate to the Monitor data screen, where you can see the data in both graphical representation and Table view. 

     

    The following are the options you can find in the Monitor data collection screen.

    • Approve: You can accept the value (collected or calculated) and provide a short description in the Comment field to explain your decision.
    • Replace: You can replace an incorrect or missing value and provide a short description in the Comment field to explain your decision.
    • Invalidate: If you consider a data record's value incorrect and do not want to use it further, you can invalidate it.
    • Create Deviation Incident: You can create a deviation incident for a selected data record (collected or calculated data) and investigate its reason as part of another process.
    • Export Data: You can export the collected, calculated, and Aggregated data into the spreadsheets.
    • Import Data: Import the collected, calculated, and aggregate data into the spreadsheets.

 

Environmental Data Explorer

  1. Choose the Environmental Data Explorer App        

  2. Environmental Data Explorer app helps you to monitor the environmental data (collected, calculated, sampled, or aggregated) at a given location.

    By default, the app loads and displays environmental data for the location (and its subordinate locations) that is set as My Location.

    Choose a location that has environmental data that you want to display

    Under the Filter section, select or deselect the criteria that you wish. The app automatically loads and displays the existing data records.

  3. You can filter data by the following attributes:

    • Issue Type: Displays results of system checks against environmental limits, and data by availability.
    • Data Status: Displays data based on its applicability and validity.
    • Year: Displays environmental data every year.
    • Month: Displays environmental data every year.
    • Day: Displays environmental data every year.
    • Data Type: Displays data by compliance scenario activity type that specifies how it was recorded in the system.
    • Activity Name: Displays the name of the compliance scenario activity.
    • Subject: Displays data by the subject of the data records in the system.
    • Activity Name: Displays the name of the compliance scenario activity.
    • Data Title: Displays data by the names of the data records in the system.
    • Location: Displays all data by location.
    • Preliminary Result: Displays data records based on preliminary results of calculations and aggregations.
    • External Source: Displays data records imported via automatic data collection from another system.      

Query Browser

  • Choose Custom Analytical Queries App                                        

     

  • In the Custom Analytical Queries App,
    • Click the “Create” button to create a custom analytical query with an appropriate Query name.
    • Select a Data Source: Choose the appropriate data source, such as the Cube CDS View - I_EHSAmountsCube. 

  •  In the General Tab: Provide an appropriate label name.

  • In the Field Selection Tab, identify and select the necessary fields for your analysis(using the Check box). The required fields are Year, Current Date, Language Key, Main Location ID, Activity ID, Activity Name, Status, Date, Year and Month, and Amount Value( Optionally, add further fields for a more comprehensive analysis).
  • In the Display tab: Optionally, Override Labels with appropriate names.
    Adjust Field Settings: Review and adjust the settings for each field as needed.
  • In the Filters tab: Optionally, set fixed or user-defined filters.
    Fixed Values: Language Key = EN, Status = 02.
    User-defined: Mandatory* (Current Date, Main Location ID, Activity ID), Year, Year and Month
  • Save Draft: Save your work as a draft.
    Preview Results (Optional): preview the results to ensure accuracy.
    Publish: Finalize and publish your analysis.                                          

  • Open Query Browser App: Launch the Query Browser application.

    Search for Published Analytical View: Locate the desired analytical view that has been published.
    Open for Analysis: Click "Open for Analysis" to begin your analysis.          

  • Enter Required Parameters: In the Analytics screen, input the necessary parameters such as Current Date, Location ID, Activity ID, and Year. Then, click on "Go".

    Fetch and Display Data: The query will retrieve and display the data in graphical charts.

  • Change Chart Type (Optional): Use the Chart Settings option located on the right side of the chart to change the chart type as needed.

  • Save Analytics (Optional): Use the options available in the top-right corner of the screen to:

     - Save the analytics as a tile.
     - Send an email.
     - Copy the URL.

entry.title: Do you know the impact YOU are having on SAP’s AI roadmap?
entry.content:
SAP is delivering AI use cases with each release – and maybe you’re wondering about how we’re prioritizing the cases we deliver.

An extremely critical input to SAP’s AI roadmap is YOU - and how you’re using SAP Cloud ERP software today.

SAP Cloud ERP customers have the chance to provide feedback in several ways:

1) New ideas for improvements can be made via the Customer Influence Portal
2) Service requests and usage information is available in SAP for me
3) Joining an industry-specific customer advisory board or influence council

SAP Finance engineering closely monitors these channels. But in addition to evaluating which requests will be added as new features on the roadmap, we’re also constantly analyzing how you are using SAP’s core finance processes – from record-to-report to finance operations – to find use cases where SAP Business AI can make your system faster-to-adopt and easier-to-use.

I’d like to highlight 2 specific generally-available AI use cases that originated from this kind of usage analysis:

  • US Tax Configuration

Configuring US sales and use taxes can be difficult for any accountant; there is a lot of complexity with 50 states and lower-level jurisdictions taking different approaches. SAP Engineering saw this as a great use case for providing pro-active guidance to set the system up correctly.

SAP Cloud ERP now uses an SAP Business AI-supported approach to walk accountants through the configuration process, smoothing onboarding for companies doing business in the US. The system uses an interactive approach to give the accountant a co-pilot that detects and generates proposals for the correct compliance configurations.

The accountant enters a US zip code, and the sales and use tax rates are proposed. A direct link to the state tax authority website provides additional confidence in the proposals – users can verify the source with a single click.

  • Depreciation key explanations

As companies extend their Cloud ERP usage to additional countries, they must update the settings for country-specific accounting treatments for fixed asset depreciation. This can be a challenge – specifying the right keys during implementation is critical to making sure the downstream calculations are accurate. And the process of doing the research and then making the correct settings can take hours.

By using SAP Business AI to explain the depreciation key setup, companies are seeing a 90% reduction in effort related to research and analysis on the deprecation configurations. New accountants can be onboarded faster, and Cloud ERP rollouts to additional countries can stay on-track.

What's next?

With each release of Cloud ERP, SAP is delivering new functional capabilities with SAP Business AI, as well as new analytics cases. But we are also constantly exploring how system usage analysis can help us deliver AI-supported capabilities that will protect your project timelines and deliver on our promise of an AI-enhanced user experience. 

One area that’s in the focus of process analysis today is accruals automation. SAP can observe posting patterns – and define AI-approaches that will add automation in future releases. Here we see huge potential in agentic approaches that proactively monitor business situations and tigger complex postings, increasing automation and reducing error rates. But more to come on “agentic in Finance” in a future blog.

You can always see our plans in the SAP Roadmap.

Want to learn about what’s available today? Explore the Discovery Center!

And here’s a “Pro Tip”: filter for the Product “#SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition”.

entry.title: Introduction to SAP DRC and Integration aspects
entry.content:
SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (SAP DRC) enables the organization to create, process, and monitor electronic documents and statutory reports. As a result, the company stays compliant with local legal obligations.

In this blog I will refer this as DRC or SAP DRC

Core Features of SAP DRC are

  1. E-Invoices for Sales
  2. Incoming Invoices
  3. Transport Registrations
  4. Legal/Statutory reporting based on country requirements

                 

Core features (in detail)

  • SAP DRC is offered on premise and on cloud and the integration with the tax authorities is standard content delivered by SAP.
  • Transport Registration is mandatory in some countries like Portugal, Romania etc so this feature is supported for relevant countires
  • Tax reporting is supported for most of the countries (NOT ALL) so ensure you check this link before assuming – SAP HELP
  • SAP DRC on Cloud license is based on volumes of invoices per month along with the countries in scope for an organization. Details can only be validated with SAP account manager.

Solution brief

Now we will discuss the process flow for both outgoing and incoming invoices

Outgoing (Sales) invoice Processing

E-Doc Cockpit view (Transaction EDOC_COCKPIT)

XML view

Transport Order generation

Incoming (purchase) invoice Processing

Statutory/Legal Reporting

Country specific reports can be generated from SAP DRC. These can be

  • VAT reports
  • SAF-T reports
  • EC Sales list
  • Many more based on country requirements

Process flow

Relevant Fiori apps

Is it on Premise or on cloud?

  1. In the business systems to make compliance a seamless step within the business processes, with no data redundancy and no data replication. This is On-Premises
  1. On SAP Business Technology Platform to connect to govt authorities and platforms. This can be SAP DRC Cloud.

For more detailed insights on SAP Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) and the cloud edition, below links can be referred

  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud edition – Online Help Documentation
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition and SAP S/4HANA – Onlin...
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition – Online Help Documentat...
  • SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for SAP ERP
  • Overview video: Integration with Peppol network with SAP Document and Reporting Compliance, cloud ed...
  • Overview video: How can SAP Document and Reporting Compliance support you in meeting the new KSeF re...
  • Overview video: Exchange invoices over the Peppol network using the Cloud Edition and Concur Invoice
  • Overview video: Discover the New eOrdering Process of SAP Document and Reporting Compliance

I will share more details around country specifics in my next blog, till then enjoy reading and share your experience in comments

(some images are taken from other SAP content and the copyright remains with the owner)

entry.title: Re: Automatically add account in involved parties to lead...
entry.content:
The involved party determination logic at the Leads for Account field = Contact of Account means that when creating a new Lead, if you select a value in the Contact field of an empty Lead document, the Account field will be automatically populated with the main Account associated with that Contact.

However, your request does not clearly specify the exact requirement you are trying to achieve. Could you clarify whether you expect the Account to be automatically added to the Involved Parties without selecting a Contact, or if you are looking for a different type of determination logic?

entry.title: Re: READ method not executing in RAP Web API V4
entry.content:
Hi,

In the handler class, hope it is explicitly maintained (the read operation), I mean something like below: -

CLASS zcl_my_handler IMPLEMENTATION.

  METHOD read_entity.
    DATA: lt_data TYPE TABLE OF zmy_entity.

    TRY.
        SELECT * FROM zmy_entity
          INTO TABLE lt_data
          WHERE key_field = @Keys-key_field.  " Use your key field

        IF lt_data IS INITIAL.
          RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception
            EXPORTING textid = /iwbep/cx_mgw_busi_exception=>business_error
                      message = 'Record not found!'.
        ENDIF.

        result = lt_data.  " Return the result

      CATCH cx_sy_open_sql_db INTO DATA(lx_sql).
        RAISE EXCEPTION TYPE /iwbep/cx_mgw_tech_exception
          EXPORTING message = lx_sql->get_text( ).

    ENDTRY.

  ENDMETHOD.

ENDCLASS.

and once this is there, can be please put a BP in this and see?

Thanks,

Arun

entry.title: Re: How to copy data from parent node to child node via d...
entry.content:
Mapping rule can be defined based on attributes also.

 

Even the Advanced Formula will work. Make sure you define in CONFIG statement for hierarchy for profit center to be set as Alternate hierarchy. Also the alternate hierarchy should also have the leaf members A,B,C,D either as root or defined as a leaf. If define as root, Include CONFIG to include members not in hierarchy in AF. Also set your hierarchy setting in dimensions to group orphan under not in hierarchy.

 

Nikhil

entry.title: Re: How to stop Source HU proposal during Allocation Ware...
entry.content:
Hi prasan_desi,

Sorry this is the standard behaviour of Sock distribution to Outbound ( Actual Picking to Outbound Delivery) thats why you used 2 step Process, however there is another option to optimize. What is your version ?

1 > Use Optimizer settings - HU will be intermidieate location where allocation stock to Outbound so business has less option. Move rest picked HU from withdraw to Staging/repacking...I totally agree you and business, it would very difficult to find the right HU where they would break so addition of this process would help business. Addition to this, you can divide bin location to sub division means Make it multiple bins in stead of 1 and keep 1 or 2 HUs max  per sub divided bin for better visibility. It definitely improve process,I know this need to work closely with business. In this case business does not need much search of HU...

 

2 > Other option is enhancement in RF if business is not agreed in option 1. 

Please let us know if any questions...

Kind Regards,

Ajit

entry.title: Work Zone search results: content pages that also appear in custom nav appear twice
entry.content:
In my WZ site I have a workpage that also appears in the custom nav.

When I search for this page it appears in results twice - see attached. 

One result is listed as a "Workpage", which when clicked presents the page in it's workpage view, without the navigation I've built. I don't want this search result to display. In general I don't ever want to expose my users to the content directory or content view. This is not how web pages behave. 

The other, confusingly, is listed as "Workspace tabs", which displays the page with the page navigation. I DO want this result to appear. 

How can I control search results to prevent this pointless and distracting duplication?

Why does this even happen?
